title: lippia strigulosa f. parvifolia
intro: Lippia strigulosa f. parvifolia, also known as small-leaved lippia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, colds, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator effects, which help to relieve congestion and coughs. The leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often prepared by steeping the plant material in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
